refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation link availability at or above the customer-defined target over the agreed evaluation period.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR to explicitly include the performance metric (link availability) and the evaluation period clarifies the requirement for design and verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure link uptime statistics over the evaluation period using telemetry data and compare against the customer-defined availability target.
sub_frs: FR1: Ensure continuous network link uptime to support teleoperation.
FR2: Detect and recover from link failures promptly to maintain availability.
FR3: Monitor and report link status to relevant system components.

candidate_dps: DP1: Redundant network link architecture with failover capability.
DP2: Real-time link health monitoring and automatic recovery protocols.
DP3: Telemetry data aggregation and status reporting subsystem.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Redundant network link architecture with failover capability.

potential_sources: Link monitoring tightly coupled with failover control
Telemetry reporting dependent on link status
coupling_check.notes: DP1 logically supports FR1 and enables FR2; DP2 and DP3 are logically related but can be modularized separately to reduce coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network infrastructure supports redundancy and failover.
Risk of increased system complexity impacting maintainability.
Risk that failover mechanisms may introduce latency affecting availability.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider decomposing monitoring and reporting DPs into separate modules to reduce coupling and improve modularity.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Physical network link hardware and failover control logic embedded in vehicle systems.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface for physical and logical network connectivity supporting redundancy and failover.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Interface to send link status and health data to monitoring modules.

module_containment_scores.notes: High-level system containing overall teleoperation logic but less direct control over physical link.
Primary containment of physical network link and failover logic; minimal diffusion.
Handles teleoperation commands but less involved in physical link redundancy.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Physical network link redundancy and failover logic are best contained within the Vehicle module to minimize diffusion and maximize control.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with Teleoperation module for link monitoring functions.
Risk of insufficient interface definition between Vehicle and Teleoperation modules for link status.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Clarify interface contracts between Vehicle and Teleoperation modules for link monitoring and status reporting.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Selected DP (redundant network link architecture) directly addresses the refined FR sub-requirements and supports high availability.

diffusion_risks: Some diffusion risk exists if link monitoring is not clearly separated from failover control.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Vehicle module shows strong containment for physical link redundancy; interfaces to Teleoperation module must be well defined.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification via telemetry and network link monitoring is feasible within Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of physical link redundancy to Vehicle; monitoring and reporting interfaces allocated to Teleoperation.

recommended_revisions: Refine interface definitions between Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.
Consider modularizing monitoring and reporting functions to reduce diffusion.

decision.justification: While the decomposition and assignments are sound, interface definitions and modularization of monitoring/reporting functions require refinement to reduce diffusion and clarify ownership.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Ensure continuous network link uptime to support teleoperation.
FR2: Detect and recover from link failures promptly to maintain availability.
FR3: Monitor and report link status to relevant system components.

revise.why: To clarify and strengthen interface contracts and reduce diffusion between Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.

assumptions: Network infrastructure supports redundancy and failover.
Telemetry data is reliable for verification.
open_risks: Potential latency introduced by failover mechanisms.
Interface ambiguity between Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.
evidence_needed: Telemetry data demonstrating link availability over evaluation period.
Interface specifications between Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.
